summary: Ferocious 4/27/69 show with a smoking Dark Star and two epic Lovelights, captured on a clear recording that lets Tom Constanten’s keyboards shine
review: Fans rave about the relentless energy of this April ’69 performance – Dark Star stretches for over eight minutes, the double‑Lovelight sandwich is described as “primal” and “ferocious,” and the segues into Me & My Uncle and Sitting on Top of the World are praised.  The tape (likely a soundboard) sounds clean enough to hear Tom Constanten throughout, with even Mickey’s odd bird‑chirp moment noted, making it a highly listenable document despite no explicit source notes.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently glowing fan comments about the musical performance (especially Dark Star and Lovelight) combined with praise for clear audio give a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tom Constanten’s keyboards are audible throughout; no reports of mud, drop‑outs or major distortion. One quirky note mentions Mickey’s drum “bird chirping” sound.

poor_songs: Sitting on Top of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: A single, marathon set that treats Lovelight as a sandwich (two back‑to‑back versions), with seamless segues into Me & My Uncle and Sitting on Top of the World; the flow feels intense, relentless, and quintessentially spring ’69.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his improvisational jams (notably a Jerry jam into Me & My Uncle) and ladder/arpeggio work in Morning Dew; strong interaction with Phil.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for intense interplay with Jerry, though one reviewer notes he gets “tenuous then lost” during the Me & My Uncle segue.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s organ/piano is clearly audible throughout, a rarity for early‑69 recordings.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming even produced an odd bird‑chirping sound that listeners noted during Dark Star.
